     1	package pancake
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"encoding/json"
     6		"fmt"
     7		"log/slog"
     8		"net/http"
     9		"sync"
    10		"time"
    11	
    12		"github.com/nextlevelbuilder/goclaw/internal/bus"
    13		"github.com/nextlevelbuilder/goclaw/internal/channels"
    14		"github.com/nextlevelbuilder/goclaw/internal/store"
    15	)
    16	
    17	// Compile-time interface assertions.
    18	var (
    19		_ channels.Channel           = (*Channel)(nil)
    20		_ channels.WebhookChannel    = (*Channel)(nil)
    21		_ channels.BlockReplyChannel = (*Channel)(nil)
    22	)
    23	
    24	const (
    25		dedupTTL        = 24 * time.Hour
    26		dedupCleanEvery = 5 * time.Minute
    27		outboundEchoTTL = 45 * time.Second
    28	)
    29	
    30	// Channel implements channels.Channel and channels.WebhookChannel for Pancake (pages.fm).
    31	// One channel instance = one Pancake page, which may serve multiple platforms (FB, Zalo, IG, etc.)
    32	type Channel struct {
    33		*channels.BaseChannel
    34		config        pancakeInstanceConfig
    35		apiClient     *APIClient
    36		pageID        string
    37		pageName      string // resolved from Pancake page metadata at Start()
    38		platform      string // resolved from Pancake page metadata at Start()
    39		webhookSecret string // optional HMAC-SHA256 secret for webhook verification
    40	
    41		// dedup prevents processing duplicate webhook deliveries.
    42		dedup sync.Map // eventKey(string) → time.Time
    43	
    44		// recentOutbound suppresses short-lived webhook echoes of our own text replies.
    45		recentOutbound sync.Map // conversationID + "\x00" + normalized content → time.Time
    46	
    47		stopCh  chan struct{}
    48		stopCtx context.Context
    49		stopFn  context.CancelFunc
    50	}
    51	
    52	// New creates a Pancake Channel from parsed credentials and config.
    53	func New(cfg pancakeInstanceConfig, creds pancakeCreds,
    54		msgBus *bus.MessageBus, _ store.PairingStore) (*Channel, error) {
    55	
    56		if creds.APIKey == "" {
    57			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: api_key is required")
    58		}
    59		if creds.PageAccessToken == "" {
    60			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: page_access_token is required")
    61		}
    62		if cfg.PageID == "" {
    63			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: page_id is required")
    64		}
    65	
    66		base := channels.NewBaseChannel(channels.TypePancake, msgBus, cfg.AllowFrom)
    67		stopCtx, stopFn := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    68	
    69		ch := &Channel{
    70			BaseChannel:   base,
    71			config:        cfg,
    72			apiClient:     NewAPIClient(creds.APIKey, creds.PageAccessToken, cfg.PageID),
    73			pageID:        cfg.PageID,
    74			platform:      cfg.Platform,
    75			webhookSecret: creds.WebhookSecret,
    76			stopCh:        make(chan struct{}),
    77			stopCtx:       stopCtx,
    78			stopFn:        stopFn,
    79		}
    80	
    81		return ch, nil
    82	}
    83	
    84	// Factory creates a Pancake Channel from DB instance data.
    85	// Implements channels.ChannelFactory.
    86	func Factory(name string, creds json.RawMessage, cfg json.RawMessage,
    87		msgBus *bus.MessageBus, pairingSvc store.PairingStore) (channels.Channel, error) {
    88	
    89		var c pancakeCreds
    90		if err := json.Unmarshal(creds, &c); err != nil {
    91			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: decode credentials: %w", err)
    92		}
    93	
    94		var ic pancakeInstanceConfig
    95		if len(cfg) > 0 {
    96			if err := json.Unmarshal(cfg, &ic); err != nil {
    97				return nil, fmt.Errorf("pancake: decode config: %w", err)
    98			}
    99		}
   100	
   101		ch, err := New(ic, c, msgBus, pairingSvc)
   102		if err != nil {
   103			return nil, err
   104		}
   105		ch.SetName(name)
   106		return ch, nil
   107	}
   108	
   109	// Start connects the channel: verifies token, resolves platform, registers webhook.
   110	func (ch *Channel) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
   111		ch.MarkStarting("connecting to Pancake page")
   112	
   113		if err := ch.apiClient.VerifyToken(ctx); err != nil {
   114			ch.MarkFailed("token invalid", err.Error(), channels.ChannelFailureKindAuth, false)
   115			return err
   116		}
   117	
   118		// Resolve platform and page name from page metadata (best-effort — don't fail on this).
   119		if ch.platform == "" || ch.pageName == "" {
   120			if page, err := ch.apiClient.GetPage(ctx); err != nil {
   121				slog.Warn("pancake: could not resolve platform from page metadata", "page_id", ch.pageID, "err", err)
   122			} else {
   123				if page.Platform != "" {
   124					ch.platform = page.Platform
   125				}
   126				if page.Name != "" {
   127					ch.pageName = page.Name
   128				}
   129			}
   130		}
   131	
   132		if ch.webhookSecret == "" {
   133			slog.Warn("security.pancake_webhook_no_secret",
   134				"page_id", ch.pageID,
   135				"note", "webhook_secret not configured; incoming webhook requests will not be authenticated")
   136		}
   137	
   138		globalRouter.register(ch)
   139		ch.MarkHealthy("connected to page " + ch.pageID)
   140		ch.SetRunning(true)
   141	
   142		// Background goroutine: evict stale dedup entries to prevent memory growth.
   143		go ch.runDedupCleaner()
   144	
   145		slog.Info("pancake channel started",
   146			"page_id", ch.pageID,
   147			"platform", ch.platform,
   148			"name", ch.Name())
   149		return nil
   150	}
   151	
   152	// Stop gracefully shuts down the channel.
   153	func (ch *Channel) Stop(_ context.Context) error {
   154		globalRouter.unregister(ch.pageID)
   155		ch.stopFn()
   156		close(ch.stopCh)
   157		ch.SetRunning(false)
   158		ch.MarkStopped("stopped")
   159		slog.Info("pancake channel stopped", "page_id", ch.pageID, "name", ch.Name())
   160		return nil
   161	}
   162	
   163	// Send delivers an outbound message via Pancake API.
   164	func (ch *Channel) Send(ctx context.Context, msg bus.OutboundMessage) error {
   165		slog.Debug("pancake: Send called",
   166			"page_id", ch.pageID,
   167			"chat_id", msg.ChatID,
   168			"content_len", len(msg.Content),
   169			"platform", ch.platform,
   170			"channel_name", ch.Name())
   171	
   172		conversationID := msg.ChatID
   173		if conversationID == "" {
   174			return fmt.Errorf("pancake: chat_id (conversation_id) is required for outbound message")
   175		}
   176	
   177		text := FormatOutbound(msg.Content, ch.platform)
   178	
   179		// Handle media attachments.
   180		attachmentIDs, err := ch.handleMediaAttachments(ctx, msg)
   181		if err != nil {
   182			slog.Warn("pancake: media upload failed, sending text only",
   183				"page_id", ch.pageID, "err", err)
   184		}
   185	
   186		// Pancake's official contract makes `message` and `content_ids` mutually exclusive.
   187		// Deliver uploaded media first, then follow with text chunks if needed.
   188		if len(attachmentIDs) > 0 {
   189			if err := ch.apiClient.SendAttachmentMessage(ctx, conversationID, attachmentIDs); err != nil {
   190				ch.handleAPIError(err)
   191				return err
   192			}
   193			if text == "" {
   194				return nil
   195			}
   196		}
   197	
   198		// Text-only: split into platform-appropriate chunks.
   199		// Store echo fingerprints BEFORE sending so that webhook echoes arriving
   200		// while the HTTP round-trip is in flight are already recognized as self-sent.
   201		parts := splitMessage(text, ch.maxMessageLength())
   202		for _, part := range parts {
   203			ch.rememberOutboundEcho(conversationID, part)
   204		}
   205		for _, part := range parts {
   206			if err := ch.apiClient.SendMessage(ctx, conversationID, part); err != nil {
   207				ch.handleAPIError(err)
   208				// Remove pre-stored echo fingerprints for unsent parts so they
   209				// don't suppress genuine customer messages that happen to match.
   210				ch.forgetOutboundEcho(conversationID, part)
   211				return err
   212			}
   213		}
   214		return nil
   215	}
   216	
   217	// BlockReplyEnabled returns the per-channel block_reply override (nil = inherit gateway default).
   218	func (ch *Channel) BlockReplyEnabled() *bool { return ch.config.BlockReply }
   219	
   220	// WebhookHandler returns the shared webhook path and global router as handler.
   221	// Only the first pancake instance mounts the route; others return ("", nil).
   222	func (ch *Channel) WebhookHandler() (string, http.Handler) {
   223		return globalRouter.webhookRoute()
   224	}
   225	
   226	// handleAPIError maps Pancake API errors to channel health states.
   227	func (ch *Channel) handleAPIError(err error) {
   228		if err == nil {
   229			return
   230		}
   231		switch {
   232		case isAuthError(err):
   233			ch.MarkFailed("token expired or invalid", err.Error(), channels.ChannelFailureKindAuth, false)
   234		case isRateLimitError(err):
   235			ch.MarkDegraded("rate limited", err.Error(), channels.ChannelFailureKindNetwork, true)
   236		default:
   237			ch.MarkDegraded("api error", err.Error(), channels.ChannelFailureKindUnknown, true)
   238		}
   239	}
   240	
   241	// maxMessageLength returns the platform-specific character limit.
   242	func (ch *Channel) maxMessageLength() int {
   243		switch ch.platform {
   244		case "tiktok":
   245			return 500
   246		case "instagram":
   247			return 1000
   248		case "facebook", "zalo":
   249			return 2000
   250		case "whatsapp":
   251			return 4096
   252		case "line":
   253			return 5000
   254		default:
   255			return 2000
   256		}
   257	}
   258

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /workspace/goclaw/internal/channels/pancake/webhook_handler.go:
     1	package pancake
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"crypto/hmac"
     5		"crypto/sha256"
     6		"encoding/hex"
     7		"encoding/json"
     8		"io"
     9		"log/slog"
    10		"net/http"
    11		"strings"
    12		"sync"
    13	)
    14	
    15	const (
    16		webhookPath  = "/channels/pancake/webhook"
    17		maxBodyBytes = 1 << 20 // 1 MB — prevent abuse
    18	)
    19	
    20	// verifyHMAC verifies a Pancake HMAC-SHA256 signature.
    21	// Expected header format: "sha256=<hex-digest>"
    22	func verifyHMAC(body []byte, secret, signature string) bool {
    23		const prefix = "sha256="
    24		if len(signature) <= len(prefix) {
    25			return false
    26		}
    27		got, err := hex.DecodeString(signature[len(prefix):])
    28		if err != nil {
    29			return false
    30		}
    31		mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
    32		mac.Write(body)
    33		expected := mac.Sum(nil)
    34		return hmac.Equal(got, expected)
    35	}
    36	
    37	// --- Global webhook router for multi-page support ---
    38	
    39	// webhookRouter routes incoming Pancake webhook events to the correct channel instance by page_id.
    40	// A single HTTP handler is shared across all pancake channel instances.
    41	type webhookRouter struct {
    42		mu           sync.RWMutex
    43		instances    map[string]*Channel // pageID → channel
    44		routeHandled bool                // true after first webhookRoute() call
    45	}
    46	
    47	var globalRouter = &webhookRouter{
    48		instances: make(map[string]*Channel),
    49	}
    50	
    51	func (r *webhookRouter) register(ch *Channel) {
    52		r.mu.Lock()
    53		defer r.mu.Unlock()
    54		r.instances[ch.pageID] = ch
    55	}
    56	
    57	func (r *webhookRouter) unregister(pageID string) {
    58		r.mu.Lock()
    59		defer r.mu.Unlock()
    60		delete(r.instances, pageID)
    61	}
    62	
    63	// webhookRoute returns the path+handler on first call; ("", nil) for subsequent calls.
    64	// The HTTP mux retains the route once registered — routeHandled prevents duplicate mounts.
    65	func (r *webhookRouter) webhookRoute() (string, http.Handler) {
    66		r.mu.Lock()
    67		defer r.mu.Unlock()
    68		if !r.routeHandled {
    69			r.routeHandled = true
    70			return webhookPath, r
    71		}
    72		return "", nil
    73	}
    74	
    75	// ServeHTTP is the shared handler for all Pancake page webhooks.
    76	// Always returns HTTP 200 — Pancake suspends webhooks if >80% errors in a 30-min window.
    77	func (r *webhookRouter) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
    78		slog.Info("pancake: webhook request received",
    79			"method", req.Method,
    80			"remote_addr", req.RemoteAddr,
    81			"content_length", req.ContentLength)
    82	
    83		if req.Method != http.MethodPost {
    84			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
    85			return
    86		}
    87	
    88		lr := io.LimitReader(req.Body, maxBodyBytes+1)
    89		body, err := io.ReadAll(lr)
    90		if err != nil {
    91			slog.Warn("pancake: router read body error", "err", err)
    92			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
    93			return
    94		}
    95	
    96		slog.Debug("pancake: webhook body received",
    97			"body_len", len(body),
    98			"body_preview", truncateBody(body, 1000))
    99	
   100		// Parse top-level envelope.
   101		var event WebhookEvent
   102		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &event); err != nil {
   103			slog.Warn("pancake: router parse event error", "err", err, "body_preview", truncateBody(body, 300))
   104			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   105			return
   106		}
   107	
   108		// Parse the nested "data" object containing conversation + message.
   109		var data WebhookData
   110		if err := json.Unmarshal(event.Data, &data); err != nil {
   111			slog.Warn("pancake: router parse data error", "err", err)
   112			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   113			return
   114		}
   115	
   116		// Resolve page_id: try top-level first, then data-level, then extract from conversation ID.
   117		pageID := event.PageID
   118		if pageID == "" {
   119			pageID = data.PageID
   120		}
   121		if pageID == "" {
   122			// Conversation ID format: "pageID_senderID" — extract page portion.
   123			if idx := strings.Index(data.Conversation.ID, "_"); idx > 0 {
   124				pageID = data.Conversation.ID[:idx]
   125			}
   126		}
   127	
   128		// Resolve conversation type — only process INBOX messages.
   129		convType := strings.ToUpper(data.Conversation.Type)
   130	
   131		if event.EventType != "" && !strings.EqualFold(event.EventType, "messaging") {
   132			slog.Debug("pancake: skipping non-messaging webhook event",
   133				"event_type", event.EventType,
   134				"page_id", pageID)
   135			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   136			return
   137		}
   138	
   139		slog.Debug("pancake: webhook event parsed",
   140			"event_type", event.EventType,
   141			"page_id", pageID,
   142			"conv_id", data.Conversation.ID,
   143			"conv_type", convType,
   144			"sender_id", data.Conversation.From.ID,
   145			"sender_name", data.Conversation.From.Name,
   146			"msg_id", data.Message.ID)
   147	
   148		if pageID == "" {
   149			slog.Warn("pancake: could not determine page_id from webhook payload")
   150			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   151			return
   152		}
   153		if convType != "INBOX" {
   154			slog.Info("pancake: skipping non-inbox conversation event",
   155				"page_id", pageID,
   156				"conv_id", data.Conversation.ID,
   157				"conv_type", convType,
   158				"msg_id", data.Message.ID)
   159			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   160			return
   161		}
   162	
   163		r.mu.RLock()
   164		target := r.instances[pageID]
   165		r.mu.RUnlock()
   166	
   167		if target == nil {
   168			// Log all registered page IDs for debugging.
   169			r.mu.RLock()
   170			var registered []string
   171			for pid := range r.instances {
   172				registered = append(registered, pid)
   173			}
   174			r.mu.RUnlock()
   175			slog.Warn("pancake: no channel instance for page_id",
   176				"page_id", pageID,
   177				"registered_pages", registered)
   178			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   179			return
   180		}
   181	
   182		// HMAC signature verification — skip if webhook_secret not configured.
   183		if target.webhookSecret != "" {
   184			sig := req.Header.Get("X-Pancake-Signature")
   185			if !verifyHMAC(body, target.webhookSecret, sig) {
   186				slog.Warn("security.pancake_webhook_signature_mismatch",
   187					"page_id", pageID,
   188					"remote_addr", req.RemoteAddr)
   189				w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   190				return
   191			}
   192		}
   193	
   194		// Build normalized MessagingData from actual Pancake payload.
   195		msgContent := data.Message.Message
   196		if msgContent == "" {
   197			msgContent = data.Message.OriginalMessage
   198		}
   199		if msgContent == "" {
   200			msgContent = data.Message.Content
   201		}
   202		if msgContent == "" {
   203			msgContent = data.Conversation.Snippet
   204		}
   205	
   206		senderID := data.Conversation.From.ID
   207		senderName := data.Conversation.From.Name
   208		if data.Message.From != nil {
   209			if data.Message.From.ID != "" {
   210				senderID = data.Message.From.ID
   211			}
   212			if data.Message.From.Name != "" {
   213				senderName = data.Message.From.Name
   214			}
   215		}
   216	
   217		normalized := MessagingData{
   218			PageID:         pageID,
   219			ConversationID: data.Conversation.ID,
   220			Type:           convType,
   221			Platform:       target.platform,
   222			AssigneeIDs:    append([]string(nil), data.Conversation.AssigneeIDs...),
   223			Message: MessagingMessage{
   224				ID:          data.Message.ID,
   225				Content:     msgContent,
   226				SenderID:    senderID,
   227				SenderName:  senderName,
   228				Attachments: data.Message.Attachments,
   229			},
   230		}
   231	
   232		target.handleMessagingEvent(normalized)
   233		w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
   234	}
   235	
   236	// truncateBody returns a string preview of body, truncated to maxLen bytes.
   237	func truncateBody(body []byte, maxLen int) string {
   238		if len(body) <= maxLen {
   239			return string(body)
   240		}
   241		return string(body[:maxLen]) + "..."
   242	}

    12	// handleMessagingEvent converts a Pancake "messaging" webhook event to bus.InboundMessage.
    13	func (ch *Channel) handleMessagingEvent(data MessagingData) {
    14		slog.Debug("pancake: handleMessagingEvent called",
    15			"page_id", ch.pageID,
    16			"sender_id", data.Message.SenderID,
    17			"conversation_id", data.ConversationID,

    19			"platform", data.Platform,
    20			"msg_id", data.Message.ID,
    21			"content_len", len(data.Message.Content))
    22	
    23		// Dedup by message ID to handle Pancake's at-least-once delivery.
    24		// Skip dedup when message ID is empty — prevents shared slot "msg:" from
    25		// silently dropping all subsequent empty-ID messages across conversations.
    26		var dedupKey string
    27		if data.Message.ID != "" {
    28			dedupKey = fmt.Sprintf("msg:%s", data.Message.ID)
    29			if ch.isDup(dedupKey) {
    30				slog.Info("pancake: duplicate message skipped", "msg_id", data.Message.ID)
    31				return
    32			}
    33		}
    34	
    35		// Prevent reply loops: skip messages sent by the page itself.
    36		if data.Message.SenderID == ch.pageID {
    37			slog.Info("pancake: skipping own page message",
    38				"page_id", ch.pageID,
    39				"sender_id", data.Message.SenderID)
    40			return
    41		}
    42		if isAssignedStaff(data.AssigneeIDs, data.Message.SenderID) {
    43			slog.Info("pancake: skipping assigned staff message",
    44				"page_id", ch.pageID,
    45				"sender_id", data.Message.SenderID,
    46				"conversation_id", data.ConversationID)
    47			return
    48		}
    49	
    50		if data.Message.SenderID == "" {
    51			slog.Warn("pancake: message missing sender_id, skipping", "msg_id", data.Message.ID)
    52			return
    53		}
    54	
    55		// Check echo BEFORE buildMessageContent adds the [From: ...] prefix.
    56		// rememberOutboundEcho stores the raw outbound text; the prefix would cause a
    57		// key mismatch and silently break loop detection.
    58		if ch.isRecentOutboundEcho(data.ConversationID, data.Message.Content) {
    59			slog.Info("pancake: skipping recent outbound echo",
    60				"page_id", ch.pageID,
    61				"conversation_id", data.ConversationID,
    62				"msg_id", data.Message.ID)
    63			return
    64		}
    65	
    66		content := buildMessageContent(data)
    67	
    68		metadata := map[string]string{
    69			"pancake_mode":      strings.ToLower(data.Type), // "inbox" or "comment"
    70			"conversation_type": data.Type,
    71			"platform":          data.Platform,
    72			"conversation_id":   data.ConversationID,
    73			"message_id":        dedupKey,
    74			"display_name":      channels.SanitizeDisplayName(data.Message.SenderName),
    75			"page_name":         ch.pageName,
    76		}
    77	
    78		ch.HandleMessage(
    79			data.Message.SenderID,
    80			data.ConversationID, // ChatID = conversation_id for reply routing
    81			content,
    82			nil, // media handled inline via content URLs
    83			metadata,
    84			"direct", // Pancake inbox conversations are always treated as direct messages
    85		)
    86	
    87		slog.Debug("pancake: inbound message published to bus",
    88			"page_id", ch.pageID,
    89			"conv_id", data.ConversationID,
    90			"sender_id", data.Message.SenderID,
    91			"platform", data.Platform,
    92			"type", data.Type,
    93			"channel_name", ch.Name(),
    94		)
    95	}
    96	
    97	// buildMessageContent combines text content and attachment URLs into a single string.
    98	// Format: [From: {SenderID} ({SenderName})] {content}
    99	func buildMessageContent(data MessagingData) string {
   100		parts := []string{}
   101	
   102		if data.Message.Content != "" {
   103			parts = append(parts, stripHTML(data.Message.Content))
   104		}
   105	
   106		for _, att := range data.Message.Attachments {
   107			if att.URL != "" {
   108				parts = append(parts, att.URL)
   109			}
   110		}
   111	
   112		body := strings.Join(parts, "\n")
   113	
   114		if data.Message.SenderID != "" {
   115			prefix := fmt.Sprintf("[From: %s (%s)]", data.Message.SenderID, data.Message.SenderName)
   116			if body != "" {
   117				return prefix + " " + body
   118			}
   119			return prefix
   120		}
   121		return body
   122	}
   123	
   124	// stripHTML removes HTML tags and unescapes HTML entities from s.
   125	func stripHTML(s string) string {
   126		s = htmlTagRe.ReplaceAllString(s, "")
   127		return html.UnescapeString(strings.TrimSpace(s))
   128	}
   129	
   130	func isAssignedStaff(assigneeIDs []string, senderID string) bool {
   131		if senderID == "" {
   132			return false
   133		}
   134		for _, assigneeID := range assigneeIDs {
   135			if assigneeID == senderID {
   136				return true
   137			}
   138		}
   139		return false
   140	}

     1	package pancake
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"regexp"
     5		"strings"
     6	)
     7	
     8	// FormatOutbound formats agent response text for the target platform.
     9	// Each platform has different formatting rules and supported markup.
    10	func FormatOutbound(content string, platform string) string {
    11		switch platform {
    12		case "facebook":
    13			return formatForFacebook(content)
    14		case "whatsapp":
    15			return formatForWhatsApp(content)
    16		case "zalo", "instagram", "line":
    17			return stripMarkdown(content)
    18		case "tiktok":
    19			return stripMarkdown(truncateForTikTok(content))
    20		default:
    21			return stripMarkdown(content)
    22		}
    23	}
    24	
    25	// formatForFacebook allows basic HTML tags supported by Messenger.
    26	// Strips unsupported tags, keeps bold/italic/links.
    27	func formatForFacebook(content string) string {
    28		// Convert markdown bold (**text** or __text__) to plain (FB Messenger uses plain text)
    29		content = reBold.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    30		content = reItalic.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    31		// Strip markdown code blocks and inline code
    32		content = reCodeBlock.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    33		content = reInlineCode.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    34		// Strip markdown headers (## Heading → Heading)
    35		content = reHeader.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    36		return strings.TrimSpace(content)
    37	}
    38	
    39	// formatForWhatsApp converts markdown to WhatsApp-native formatting.
    40	// WhatsApp uses *bold*, _italic_, ~strikethrough~, ```code```.
    41	func formatForWhatsApp(content string) string {
    42		// Convert **bold** → *bold* (WhatsApp format)
    43		content = reDoubleBold.ReplaceAllString(content, "*$1*")
    44		// Convert __italic__ → _italic_ (already matches WA format, just clean up __)
    45		content = reDoubleUnderline.ReplaceAllString(content, "_$1_")
    46		// Strip markdown headers
    47		content = reHeader.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    48		// Strip inline code backticks (keep content)
    49		content = reInlineCode.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    50		return strings.TrimSpace(content)
    51	}
    52	
    53	// stripMarkdown removes common markdown formatting, returning plain text.
    54	func stripMarkdown(content string) string {
    55		content = reBold.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    56		content = reItalic.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    57		content = reCodeBlock.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    58		content = reInlineCode.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    59		content = reHeader.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    60		content = reLink.ReplaceAllString(content, "$1")
    61		content = reImage.ReplaceAllString(content, "")
    62		return strings.TrimSpace(content)
    63	}
    64	
    65	// truncateForTikTok truncates content to TikTok DM limit (500 runes).
    66	// Uses rune slicing to avoid corrupting multi-byte UTF-8 (CJK, Vietnamese, emoji).
    67	func truncateForTikTok(content string) string {
    68		const limit = 500
    69		runes := []rune(content)
    70		if len(runes) <= limit {
    71			return content
    72		}
    73		return string(runes[:limit-3]) + "..."
    74	}
    75	
    76	// Compiled regexes for markdown stripping — package-level for efficiency.
    77	var (
    78		reBold           = regexp.MustCompile(`(?:\*\*|__)(.+?)(?:\*\*|__)`)
    79		reDoubleBold     = regexp.MustCompile(`\*\*(.+?)\*\*`)
    80		reDoubleUnderline = regexp.MustCompile(`__(.+?)__`)
    81		reItalic         = regexp.MustCompile(`(?:\*|_)(.+?)(?:\*|_)`)
    82		reCodeBlock      = regexp.MustCompile("(?s)```(?:[a-z]*)?\n?(.+?)```")
    83		reInlineCode     = regexp.MustCompile("`(.+?)`")
    84		reHeader         = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^#{1,6}\s+(.+)$`)
    85		reLink           = regexp.MustCompile(`\[([^\]]+)\]\([^)]+\)`)
    86		reImage          = regexp.MustCompile(`!\[[^\]]*\]\([^)]+\)`)
    87	)

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /workspace/goclaw/internal/channels/facebook/comment_handler.go:
     1	package facebook
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"fmt"
     5		"log/slog"
     6		"strings"
     7	)
     8	
     9	// handleCommentEvent processes a feed webhook change where item == "comment".
    10	func (ch *Channel) handleCommentEvent(entry WebhookEntry, change ChangeValue) {
    11		// Feature gate.
    12		if !ch.config.Features.CommentReply {
    13			return
    14		}
    15	
    16		// Only process new comments (not edits or deletions).
    17		if change.Verb != "add" {
    18			return
    19		}
    20	
    21		// Page routing: ensure this event belongs to our page instance (before dedup write).
    22		if entry.ID != ch.pageID {
    23			return
    24		}
    25	
    26		// Self-reply prevention: skip comments posted by the page itself.
    27		if change.From.ID == ch.pageID {
    28			return
    29		}
    30	
    31		// Dedup: Facebook may deliver the same event more than once.
    32		if ch.isDup("comment:" + change.CommentID) {
    33			slog.Debug("facebook: duplicate comment event skipped", "comment_id", change.CommentID)
    34			return
    35		}
    36	
    37		// Build message content — optionally enriched with post + thread context.
    38		// Use stopCtx so inflight Graph API calls are cancelled when the channel stops.
    39		content := change.Message
    40		if ch.config.CommentReplyOptions.IncludePostContext && change.PostID != "" {
    41			content = ch.buildEnrichedContent(change)
    42		}
    43	
    44		senderID := change.From.ID
    45		// Session key groups all comments by the same user on the same post.
    46		chatID := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", change.PostID, senderID)
    47	
    48		metadata := map[string]string{
    49			"comment_id":          change.CommentID,
    50			"post_id":             change.PostID,
    51			"parent_id":           change.ParentID,
    52			"sender_name":         change.From.Name,
    53			"sender_id":           senderID,
    54			"fb_mode":             "comment",
    55			"reply_to_comment_id": change.CommentID,
    56		}
    57	
    58		ch.HandleMessage(senderID, chatID, content, nil, metadata, "direct")
    59	}
    60	
    61	// buildEnrichedContent fetches post content and comment thread, assembles a rich
    62	// context string for the agent. Uses stopCtx so it is cancelled on channel Stop().
    63	func (ch *Channel) buildEnrichedContent(change ChangeValue) string {
    64		ctx := ch.stopCtx
    65	
    66		var sb strings.Builder
    67	
    68		// Post content.
    69		post, err := ch.postFetcher.GetPost(ctx, change.PostID)
    70		if err == nil && post != nil && post.Message != "" {
    71			sb.WriteString("[Bài đăng] ")
    72			sb.WriteString(post.Message)
    73			sb.WriteString("\n\n")
    74		}
    75	
    76		// Comment thread (when this is a nested reply).
    77		if change.ParentID != "" {
    78			depth := ch.config.CommentReplyOptions.MaxThreadDepth
    79			if depth <= 0 {
    80				depth = 10
    81			}
    82			thread, err := ch.postFetcher.GetCommentThread(ctx, change.ParentID, depth)
    83			if err == nil && len(thread) > 0 {
    84				sb.WriteString("[Thread]\n")
    85				for _, c := range thread {
    86					sb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- %s: %s\n", c.From.Name, c.Message))
    87				}
    88				sb.WriteString("\n")
    89			}
    90		}
    91	
    92		// Current comment.
    93		sb.WriteString("[Comment mới] ")
    94		sb.WriteString(change.From.Name)
    95		sb.WriteString(": ")
    96		sb.WriteString(change.Message)
    97	
    98		return sb.String()
    99	}

   571	
   572	// ValidatePolicy logs warnings for common policy misconfigurations.
   573	// Should be called during channel initialization.
   574	func (c *BaseChannel) ValidatePolicy(dmPolicy, groupPolicy string) {
   575		if dmPolicy == "allowlist" && !c.HasAllowList() {
   576			slog.Warn("channel policy misconfiguration: dmPolicy=allowlist but allowFrom is empty — all DMs will be rejected",
   577				"channel", c.name)
   578		}
   579		if groupPolicy == "allowlist" && !c.HasAllowList() {
   580			slog.Warn("channel policy misconfiguration: groupPolicy=allowlist but allowFrom is empty — all group messages will be rejected",
   581				"channel", c.name)
   582		}
   583	}
   584	
   585	// HandleMessage creates an InboundMessage and publishes it to the bus.
   586	// This is the standard way for channels to forward received messages.
   587	// peerKind should be "direct" or "group" (see sessions.PeerDirect, sessions.PeerGroup).
   588	func (c *BaseChannel) HandleMessage(senderID, chatID, content string, media []string, metadata map[string]string, peerKind string) {
   589		// For DMs, enforce the allowlist as a safety net.
   590		// For group messages, skip this check — group access is already enforced
   591		// by the channel-specific group policy (checkGroupPolicy / CheckPolicy).
   592		// Re-checking the sender here would incorrectly block users who are not
   593		// individually listed but are in an allowed (or open-policy) group.
   594		if peerKind != "group" && !c.IsAllowed(senderID) {
   595			return
   596		}
   597	
   598		// Derive userID from senderID: strip "|username" suffix if present (legacy Slack compound format).
   599		// All channels now pass plain senderID; kept for backward compat with stored compound IDs.
   600		userID := senderID
   601		if idx := strings.IndexByte(senderID, '|'); idx > 0 {
   602			userID = senderID[:idx]
   603		}
   604	
   605		// Convert string paths to MediaFile (for channels that haven't been updated yet).
   606		var mediaFiles []bus.MediaFile
   607		for _, p := range media {
   608			mediaFiles = append(mediaFiles, bus.MediaFile{Path: p})
   609		}
   610	
   611		msg := bus.InboundMessage{
   612			Channel:  c.name,
   613			SenderID: senderID,
   614			ChatID:   chatID,
   615			Content:  content,
   616			Media:    mediaFiles,
   617			PeerKind: peerKind,
   618			UserID:   userID,
   619			Metadata: metadata,
   620			TenantID: c.tenantID,
   621			AgentID:  c.agentID,
   622		}
   623	
   624		c.bus.PublishInbound(msg)
   625	}
   626	
   627	// GroupMember represents a member of a group chat.
   628	type GroupMember struct {
   629		MemberID string `json:"member_id"`
   630		Name     string `json:"name"`
   631	}
   632	
   633	// GroupMemberProvider is optionally implemented by channels that can list group members.
   634	type GroupMemberProvider interface {
   635		ListGroupMembers(ctx context.Context, chatID string) ([]GroupMember, error)
   636	}
   637	
   638	// PendingCompactable is optionally implemented by channels that have a PendingHistory
   639	// supporting LLM-based compaction. InstanceLoader uses this to wire compaction config
   640	// after channel creation.
   641	type PendingCompactable interface {
   642		SetPendingCompaction(cfg *CompactionConfig)
   643	}
   644	
   645	// Truncate shortens a string to maxLen, appending "..." if truncated.
   646	func Truncate(s string, maxLen int) string {
   647		if len(s) <= maxLen {
   648			return s
   649		}
   650		return s[:maxLen] + "..."

task_list.title: Restate issue and identify testing constraints
task_list.notes: COMMENT webhooks with convType="COMMENT" must no longer be dropped. They should pass feature/filter/self/dedup gates, optionally enrich content with post context, run HandleMessage, then Send replies with Pancake action reply_comment and optional one-time private_reply first-inbox DM. Technical names to preserve: features.comment_reply, comment_reply_options.filter/keywords/include_post_context, features.first_inbox, PostFetcher singleflight TTL cache, echo dedup before enrichment, pancake_mode="comment". Repro: configure Pancake channel, send COMMENT webhook, observe no agent routing/reply path before fix. Testing best practices: do not edit tests, use existing Go tests, temporary reproduction only, verify dedup/filter/edge cases.

task_list.title: Run existing repository tests to establish baseline
task_list.notes: README recommends go test ./...; Makefile uses go test -race -timeout=90s ./.... Baseline go test ./... fails only in internal/http Ollama-related tests due host.docker.internal DNS/API base expectations, unrelated to Pancake channel.

task_list.title: Explore Pancake channel implementation
task_list.notes: Relevant files: internal/channels/pancake/webhook_handler.go currently drops convType != INBOX; message_handler.go can normalize/publish but lacks comment gates/metadata; pancake.go Send always uses reply_inbox; api_client.go only supports reply_inbox/upload; types.go lacks first_inbox/comment_reply_options/post/comment fields; no Pancake PostFetcher exists. Facebook comment_handler.go/post_fetcher.go provide the closest model.

task_list.title: Create temporary reproduction script
task_list.notes: /tmp/pancake_comment_repro.sh injects a temporary package-local test, verifies a COMMENT webhook with features.comment_reply=true publishes inbound metadata pancake_mode=comment and reply_to_comment_id. It fails before the fix because router logs skipping non-inbox conversation event.

task_list.title: Implement minimal non-test code changes
task_list.notes: Add COMMENT handling, post fetch/cache, send actions, and dedup/filter behavior as needed.

task_list.title: Verify reproduction and relevant tests
task_list.notes: Run the temporary script, relevant package tests, and review diffs against requirements.

Here's the result of running `cat -n` on /workspace/goclaw/internal/channels/pancake/types.go:
     1	// Package pancake implements the Pancake (pages.fm) channel for GoClaw.
     2	// Pancake acts as a unified proxy for Facebook, Zalo OA, Instagram, TikTok, WhatsApp, Line.
     3	// A single Pancake API key gives access to all connected platforms — no per-platform OAuth needed.
     4	package pancake
     5	
     6	import "encoding/json"
     7	
     8	// pancakeCreds holds encrypted credentials stored in channel_instances.credentials.
     9	type pancakeCreds struct {
    10		APIKey          string `json:"api_key"`                  // User-level Pancake API key
    11		PageAccessToken string `json:"page_access_token"`        // Page-level token for all page APIs
    12		WebhookSecret   string `json:"webhook_secret,omitempty"` // Optional HMAC-SHA256 verification
    13	}
    14	
    15	// pancakeInstanceConfig holds non-secret config from channel_instances.config JSONB.
    16	type pancakeInstanceConfig struct {
    17		PageID   string `json:"page_id"`
    18		Platform string `json:"platform,omitempty"` // auto-detected at Start(): facebook/zalo/instagram/tiktok/whatsapp/line
    19		Features struct {
    20			InboxReply   bool `json:"inbox_reply"`
    21			CommentReply bool `json:"comment_reply"`
    22		} `json:"features"`
    23		AllowFrom  []string `json:"allow_from,omitempty"`
    24		BlockReply *bool    `json:"block_reply,omitempty"` // override gateway block_reply (nil = inherit)
    25	}
    26	
    27	// --- Webhook payload types ---
    28	// These types match the actual Pancake (pages.fm) webhook delivery format.
    29	// Top-level envelope has optional "event_type" + nested "data" containing
    30	// "conversation" and "message" objects.
    31	
    32	// WebhookEvent is the top-level Pancake webhook delivery envelope.
    33	type WebhookEvent struct {
    34		EventType string          `json:"event_type,omitempty"` // "messaging", may be empty
    35		PageID    string          `json:"page_id,omitempty"`    // top-level page_id (some formats)
    36		Data      json.RawMessage `json:"data"`
    37	}
    38	
    39	// WebhookData is the "data" envelope inside a Pancake webhook event.
    40	type WebhookData struct {
    41		Conversation WebhookConversation `json:"conversation"`
    42		Message      WebhookMessage      `json:"message"`
    43		PageID       string              `json:"page_id,omitempty"` // page_id may appear here or top-level
    44	}
    45	
    46	// WebhookConversation holds the conversation metadata from a Pancake webhook.
    47	type WebhookConversation struct {
    48		ID          string        `json:"id"`                    // format: "pageID_senderID"
    49		Type        string        `json:"type"`                  // "INBOX" or "COMMENT"
    50		AssigneeIDs []string      `json:"assignee_ids,omitempty"` // Pancake staff IDs assigned to this conversation
    51		From        WebhookSender `json:"from"`
    52		Snippet     string        `json:"snippet,omitempty"`
    53	}
    54	
    55	// WebhookSender identifies the message sender.
    56	type WebhookSender struct {
    57		ID             string `json:"id"`
    58		Name           string `json:"name"`
    59		Email          string `json:"email,omitempty"`
    60		PageCustomerID string `json:"page_customer_id,omitempty"`
    61	}
    62	
    63	// WebhookMessage holds the message payload from a Pancake webhook.
    64	type WebhookMessage struct {
    65		ID              string              `json:"id"`
    66		Message         string              `json:"message,omitempty"`          // primary text content
    67		OriginalMessage string              `json:"original_message,omitempty"` // unformatted fallback
    68		Content         string              `json:"content,omitempty"`          // legacy field
    69		From            *WebhookSender      `json:"from,omitempty"`             // actual sender of this message
    70		Attachments     []MessageAttachment `json:"attachments,omitempty"`
    71		CreatedAt       json.Number         `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
    72	}
    73	
    74	// MessageAttachment represents a media attachment in a Pancake webhook message.
    75	type MessageAttachment struct {
    76		Type string `json:"type"` // "image", "video", "file"
    77		URL  string `json:"url"`
    78	}
    79	
    80	// MessagingData is the normalized internal representation used after parsing.
    81	type MessagingData struct {
    82		PageID         string
    83		ConversationID string
    84		Type           string // "INBOX" or "COMMENT"
    85		Platform       string // "facebook", "zalo", "instagram", "tiktok", "whatsapp", "line"
    86		AssigneeIDs    []string
    87		Message        MessagingMessage
    88	}
    89	
    90	// MessagingMessage is the normalized message used by the handler.
    91	type MessagingMessage struct {
    92		ID          string
    93		Content     string
    94		SenderID    string
    95		SenderName  string
    96		Attachments []MessageAttachment
    97	}
    98	
    99	// --- API response types ---
   100	
   101	// PancakePost is a post object returned by Pancake list-posts APIs.
   102	type PancakePost struct {
   103	ID          string `json:"id"`
   104	PostID      string `json:"post_id,omitempty"`
   105	Message     string `json:"message,omitempty"`
   106	Content     string `json:"content,omitempty"`
   107	Description string `json:"description,omitempty"`
   108	Caption     string `json:"caption,omitempty"`
   109	Story       string `json:"story,omitempty"`
   110	}
   111	
   112	func (p PancakePost) CanonicalID() string {
   113	if p.ID != "" {
   114	return p.ID
   115	}
   116	return p.PostID
   117	}
   118	
   119	func (p PancakePost) Text() string {
   120	for _, text := range []string{p.Message, p.Content, p.Description, p.Caption, p.Story} {
   121	if text != "" {
   122	return text
   123	}
   124	}
   125	return ""
   126	}
   127	
   128	
   129	// PageInfo holds page metadata from GET /pages response.
   130	type PageInfo struct {
   131		ID       string `json:"id"`
   132		Name     string `json:"name"`
   133		Platform string `json:"platform"` // facebook/zalo/instagram/tiktok/whatsapp/line
   134		Avatar   string `json:"avatar,omitempty"`
   135	}
   136	
   137	// SendMessageRequest is the POST body for sending a message via Pancake API.
   138	type SendMessageRequest struct {
   139		Action     string   `json:"action"`
   140		Message    string   `json:"message,omitempty"`
   141		ContentIDs []string `json:"content_ids,omitempty"`
   142	}
   143	
   144	// UploadResponse is returned by POST /pages/{id}/upload_contents.
   145	type UploadResponse struct {
   146		ID  string `json:"id"`
   147		URL string `json:"url,omitempty"`
   148	}
   149	
   150	// apiError wraps a Pancake API error response.
   151	type apiError struct {
   152		Code    int    `json:"code"`
   153		Message string `json:"message"`
   154	}
   155	
   156	func (e *apiError) Error() string {
   157		return e.Message
   158	}

new = r'''// Send delivers an outbound message via Pancake API.
func (ch *Channel) Send(ctx context.Context, msg bus.OutboundMessage) error {
    slog.Debug("pancake: Send called",
        "page_id", ch.pageID,
        "chat_id", msg.ChatID,
        "content_len", len(msg.Content),
        "platform", ch.platform,
        "channel_name", ch.Name())
    if msg.Metadata["pancake_mode"] == "comment" {
        return ch.sendCommentReply(ctx, msg)
    }
    return ch.sendInboxReply(ctx, msg)
}
func (ch *Channel) sendInboxReply(ctx context.Context, msg bus.OutboundMessage) error {
    conversationID := msg.ChatID
    if conversationID == "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("pancake: chat_id (conversation_id) is required for outbound message")
    }
    text := FormatOutbound(msg.Content, ch.platform)
    // Handle media attachments.
    attachmentIDs, err := ch.handleMediaAttachments(ctx, msg)
    if err != nil {
        slog.Warn("pancake: media upload failed, sending text only",
            "page_id", ch.pageID, "err", err)
    }
    // Pancake's official contract makes `message` and `content_ids` mutually exclusive.
    // Deliver uploaded media first, then follow with text chunks if needed.
    if len(attachmentIDs) > 0 {
        if err := ch.apiClient.SendAttachmentMessage(ctx, conversationID, attachmentIDs); err != nil {
            ch.handleAPIError(err)
            return err
        }
        if text == "" {
            return nil
        }
    }
    // Text-only: split into platform-appropriate chunks.
    // Store echo fingerprints BEFORE sending so that webhook echoes arriving
    // while the HTTP round-trip is in flight are already recognized as self-sent.
    parts := splitMessage(text, ch.maxMessageLength())
    for _, part := range parts {
        ch.rememberOutboundEcho(conversationID, part)
    }
    for _, part := range parts {
        if err := ch.apiClient.SendMessage(ctx, conversationID, part); err != nil {
            ch.handleAPIError(err)
            // Remove pre-stored echo fingerprints for unsent parts so they
            // don't suppress genuine customer messages that happen to match.
            ch.forgetOutboundEcho(conversationID, part)
            return err
        }
    }
    return nil
}
func (ch *Channel) sendCommentReply(ctx context.Context, msg bus.OutboundMessage) error {
    conversationID := msg.ChatID
    if conversationID == "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("pancake: chat_id (conversation_id) is required for comment reply")
    }
    commentID := msg.Metadata["reply_to_comment_id"]
    if commentID == "" {
        commentID = msg.Metadata["comment_id"]
    }
    if commentID == "" {
        return fmt.Errorf("pancake: reply_to_comment_id missing in outbound metadata")
    }
    text := FormatOutbound(msg.Content, ch.platform)
    parts := splitMessage(text, ch.maxMessageLength())
    for _, part := range parts {
        ch.rememberOutboundEcho(conversationID, part)
    }
    for _, part := range parts {
        if err := ch.apiClient.ReplyComment(ctx, conversationID, commentID, part); err != nil {
            ch.handleAPIError(err)
            ch.forgetOutboundEcho(conversationID, part)
            return err
        }
    }
    if ch.config.Features.FirstInbox {
        ch.sendFirstInbox(ctx, conversationID, commentID, msg.Metadata["sender_id"])
    }
    return nil
}
func (ch *Channel) sendFirstInbox(ctx context.Context, conversationID, commentID, senderID string) {
    if senderID == "" {
        return
    }
    if _, alreadySent := ch.firstInboxSent.LoadOrStore(senderID, struct{}{}); alreadySent {
        return
    }
    message := ch.config.FirstInboxMessage
    if message == "" {
        message = "Cảm ơn bạn đã comment! Mình có thể hỗ trợ thêm qua tin nhắn riêng."
    }
    ch.rememberOutboundEcho(conversationID, message)
    if err := ch.apiClient.PrivateReply(ctx, conversationID, commentID, message); err != nil {
        slog.Warn("pancake: first inbox send failed", "sender_id", senderID, "err", err)
        ch.firstInboxSent.Delete(senderID)
        ch.forgetOutboundEcho(conversationID, message)
    }
}
'''
s = s[:start] + new + s[end:]
p.write_text(s)
PY

// Per-channel-type field definitions for credentials and config.
// Used by the form dialog to render proper UI fields instead of raw JSON.

export interface FieldDef {
  key: string;
  label: string;
  type: "text" | "password" | "number" | "boolean" | "select" | "tags" | "tristate" | "textarea" | "tool-select" | "skill-select";
  placeholder?: string;
  required?: boolean;
  defaultValue?: string | number | boolean | string[];
  options?: { value: string; label: string }[];
  help?: string;
  /** Only show this field when another field has a specific value */
  showWhen?: { key: string; value: string };
  /** Disable this field when another field has a specific value */
  disabledWhen?: { key: string; value: string; hint?: string };
}

// --- Shared option lists ---

const blockReplyOptions = [
  { value: "inherit", label: "Inherit from gateway" },
  { value: "true", label: "Enabled" },
  { value: "false", label: "Disabled" },
];

const dmPolicyOptions = [
  { value: "pairing", label: "Pairing (require code)" },
  { value: "open", label: "Open (accept all)" },
  { value: "allowlist", label: "Allowlist only" },
  { value: "disabled", label: "Disabled" },
];

export const groupPolicyOptions = [
  { value: "open", label: "Open (accept all)" },
  { value: "pairing", label: "Pairing (require approval)" },
  { value: "allowlist", label: "Allowlist only" },
  { value: "disabled", label: "Disabled" },
];

const mentionModeOptions = [
  { value: "strict", label: "Default (follow @mention setting)" },
  { value: "yield", label: "Multi-bot (respond unless another bot is @mentioned)" },
];

const commentFilterOptions = [
  { value: "all", label: "All comments" },
  { value: "keyword", label: "Keyword match" },
];


// --- Credentials schemas ---

export const credentialsSchema: Record<string, FieldDef[]> = {
  telegram: [
    { key: "token", label: "Bot Token", type: "password", required: true, placeholder: "123456:ABC-DEF...", help: "From @BotFather" },
  ],
  discord: [
    { key: "token", label: "Bot Token", type: "password", required: true, placeholder: "Discord bot token" },
  ],
  slack: [
    { key: "bot_token", label: "Bot Token", type: "password", required: true, placeholder: "xoxb-...", help: "Bot User OAuth Token from your Slack app's OAuth & Permissions page" },
    { key: "app_token", label: "App-Level Token", type: "password", required: true, placeholder: "xapp-...", help: "App-Level Token with connections:write scope (required for Socket Mode)" },
    { key: "user_token", label: "User Token (Optional)", type: "password", required: false, placeholder: "xoxp-...", help: "Optional: User OAuth Token for custom bot identity. Leave empty to use default bot identity." },
  ],
  feishu: [
    { key: "app_id", label: "App ID", type: "text", required: true, placeholder: "cli_xxxxx" },
    { key: "app_secret", label: "App Secret", type: "password", required: true },
    { key: "encrypt_key", label: "Encrypt Key", type: "password", help: "For webhook event decryption", showWhen: { key: "connection_mode", value: "webhook" } },
    { key: "verification_token", label: "Verification Token", type: "password", help: "For webhook event verification", showWhen: { key: "connection_mode", value: "webhook" } },
  ],
  zalo_oa: [
    { key: "token", label: "OA Access Token", type: "password", required: true },
    { key: "webhook_secret", label: "Webhook Secret", type: "password" },
  ],
  zalo_personal: [],
  whatsapp: [],
  facebook: [
    { key: "page_access_token", label: "Page Access Token", type: "password", required: true, help: "From Facebook Developer Console → Your App → Messenger → Page Access Token" },
    { key: "app_secret", label: "App Secret", type: "password", required: true, help: "From Facebook Developer Console → Your App → Settings → Basic" },
    { key: "verify_token", label: "Webhook Verify Token", type: "password", required: true, help: "A secret string you choose, used to verify the webhook URL" },
  ],
  pancake: [
    { key: "api_key", label: "API Key", type: "password", required: true, help: "Pancake user-level API key from pages.fm account settings" },
    { key: "page_access_token", label: "Page Access Token", type: "password", required: true, help: "Page-level token from Pancake dashboard → Page Settings" },
    { key: "webhook_secret", label: "Webhook Secret (Optional)", type: "password", help: "HMAC-SHA256 secret for webhook signature verification. Leave empty to skip verification." },
  ],
};

// --- Config schemas ---

export const configSchema: Record<string, FieldDef[]> = {
  telegram: [
    { key: "api_server", label: "API Server URL", type: "text", placeholder: "http://127.0.0.1:8081", help: "Custom Telegram Bot API server for large file uploads (up to 2GB). Leave empty for default." },
    { key: "proxy", label: "HTTP Proxy", type: "text", placeholder: "http://proxy:8080", help: "Route bot traffic through an HTTP proxy" },
    { key: "dm_policy", label: "DM Policy", type: "select", options: dmPolicyOptions, defaultValue: "pairing" },

// --- Group override schema (Telegram per-group/topic overrides) ---
// Uses tristate fields: undefined = inherit from parent, value = override.
// tristate without options → Inherit/Yes/No (boolean).
// tristate with options → Inherit + custom options (string).

export const groupOverrideSchema: FieldDef[] = [
  { key: "group_policy", label: "Group Policy", type: "tristate", options: groupPolicyOptions },
  { key: "mention_mode", label: "Mention Mode", type: "tristate", options: mentionModeOptions },
  { key: "require_mention", label: "Require @mention", type: "tristate", disabledWhen: { key: "mention_mode", value: "yield", hint: "fieldConfig.require_mention.disabledHint" } },
  { key: "enabled", label: "Enabled", type: "tristate" },
  { key: "allow_from", label: "Allowed Users", type: "tags", placeholder: "User IDs, one per line", help: "Restrict which users can interact in this group" },
  { key: "skills", label: "Skills Filter", type: "skill-select", help: "Limit available skills for this group" },
  { key: "tools", label: "Tool Allowlist", type: "tool-select", help: "Restrict which tools the agent can use in this group" },
  { key: "system_prompt", label: "System Prompt", type: "textarea", placeholder: "Additional system prompt for this group..." },
];

// --- Required API scopes per channel type ---
// Displayed as a help reference when creating/configuring a channel.

export interface ScopeEntry {
  scope: string;
  note?: string; // e.g. "Range: All members"
}

export const requiredScopes: Partial<Record<string, ScopeEntry[]>> = {
  feishu: [
    { scope: "application:application:self_manage" },

    { scope: "event:ip_list" },

    { scope: "im:message.p2p_msg:readonly" },
    { scope: "im:message:readonly" },

    { scope: "im:resource" },
  ],
};

// --- Post-create wizard configuration ---
// Channels with multi-step create flows (e.g. auth then config).
// Channels not listed here use the default single-step create.

export interface WizardConfig {
  /** Post-create step sequence */
  steps: ("auth" | "config")[];
  /** Custom label for the create button */
  createLabel?: string;
  /** Info banner shown on the form step during create */
  formBanner?: string;
  /** Config field keys excluded from form step (handled in wizard config step) */
  excludeConfigFields?: string[];
}

export const wizardConfig: Partial<Record<string, WizardConfig>> = {
  zalo_personal: {
    steps: ["auth", "config"],
    createLabel: "wizard.zaloPersonal.createLabel",
    formBanner: "wizard.zaloPersonal.formBanner",
    excludeConfigFields: ["allow_from"],
  },
  whatsapp: {
    steps: ["auth"],
    createLabel: "wizard.whatsapp.createLabel",
    formBanner: "wizard.whatsapp.formBanner",
  },
};

// Per-channel-type field definitions for credentials and config.
// Used by the form dialog to render proper UI fields instead of raw JSON.

export interface FieldDef {
  key: string;
  label: string;
  type: "text" | "password" | "number" | "boolean" | "select" | "tags" | "tristate" | "textarea" | "tool-select" | "skill-select";
  placeholder?: string;
  required?: boolean;
  defaultValue?: string | number | boolean | string[];
  options?: { value: string; label: string }[];
  help?: string;
  /** Only show this field when another field has a specific value */
  showWhen?: { key: string; value: string };
  /** Disable this field when another field has a specific value */
  disabledWhen?: { key: string; value: string; hint?: string };
}

// --- Shared option lists ---

const blockReplyOptions = [
  { value: "inherit", label: "Inherit from gateway" },
  { value: "true", label: "Enabled" },
  { value: "false", label: "Disabled" },
];

const dmPolicyOptions = [

  { value: "open", label: "Open (accept all)" },
  { value: "allowlist", label: "Allowlist only" },
  { value: "disabled", label: "Disabled" },
];

export const groupPolicyOptions = [
  { value: "open", label: "Open (accept all)" },

  { value: "allowlist", label: "Allowlist only" },

];

const mentionModeOptions = [
  { value: "strict", label: "Default (follow @mention setting)" },
  { value: "yield", label: "Multi-bot (respond unless another bot is @mentioned)" },
];

const commentFilterOptions = [
  { value: "all", label: "All comments" },
  { value: "keyword", label: "Keyword match" },
];

// Per-channel-type field definitions for credentials and config.
// Used by the form dialog to render proper UI fields instead of raw JSON.

export interface FieldDef {
  key: string;
  label: string;
  type: "text" | "password" | "number" | "boolean" | "select" | "tags" | "tristate" | "textarea" | "tool-select" | "skill-select";
  placeholder?: string;
  required?: boolean;
  defaultValue?: string | number | boolean | string[];
  options?: { value: string; label: string }[];
  help?: string;
  /** Only show this field when another field has a specific value */
  showWhen?: { key: string; value: string };
  /** Disable this field when another field has a specific value */
  disabledWhen?: { key: string; value: string; hint?: string };
}

// --- Shared option lists ---

const blockReplyOptions = [

];

// --- Post-create wizard configuration ---
// Channels with multi-step create flows (e.g. auth then config).
// Channels not listed here use the default single-step create.

export interface WizardConfig {
  /** Post-create step sequence */
  steps: ("auth" | "config")[];
  /** Custom label for the create button */
  createLabel?: string;
  /** Info banner shown on the form step during create */
  formBanner?: string;
  /** Config field keys excluded from form step (handled in wizard config step) */
  excludeConfigFields?: string[];
}

export const wizardConfig: Partial<Record<string, WizardConfig>> = {
  zalo_personal: {
    steps: ["auth", "config"],
    createLabel: "wizard.zaloPersonal.createLabel",
    formBanner: "wizard.zaloPersonal.formBanner",
    excludeConfigFields: ["allow_from"],
  },
  whatsapp: {
    steps: ["auth"],
    createLabel: "wizard.whatsapp.createLabel",
    formBanner: "wizard.whatsapp.formBanner",
  },
};

They should pass feature/filter/self/dedup gates, optionally enrich content with post context, run HandleMessage, then Send replies with Pancake action reply_comment and optional one-time private_reply first-inbox DM. Technical names to preserve: features.comment_reply, comment_reply_options.filter/keywords/include_post_context, features.first_inbox, PostFetcher singleflight TTL cache, echo dedup before enrichment, pancake_mode="comment". Repro: configure Pancake channel, send COMMENT webhook, observe no agent routing/reply path before fix. Testing best practices: do not edit tests, use existing Go tests, temporary reproduction only, verify dedup/filter/edge cases.

action.message:
Implemented the Pancake COMMENT auto-reply and first-inbox flow with minimal non-test changes.

Summary by requested phases:

1. Reading / problem restatement
- Pancake was parsing webhooks but explicitly dropping any `conversation.type != "INBOX"`, so `COMMENT` webhooks never reached the agent pipeline.
- Required behavior: route `COMMENT` events when `features.comment_reply` is true, optionally filter by keywords, optionally prepend post context, reply publicly via `reply_comment`, and optionally send one one-time DM via `private_reply` when `features.first_inbox` is true.
- Important technical details preserved: `pancake_mode="comment"`, `reply_to_comment_id`, `features.comment_reply`, `features.first_inbox`, `comment_reply_options.filter`, `comment_reply_options.keywords`, `comment_reply_options.include_post_context`, singleflight post-list fetch key behavior, and echo detection before content enrichment.

2. Running / baseline
- Baseline `go test ./...` failed in `internal/http` Ollama model tests because the environment resolves/normalizes Ollama URLs to `host.docker.internal`; this was already unrelated to Pancake.

3. Exploration
- Relevant files identified:
  - `internal/channels/pancake/webhook_handler.go`
  - `internal/channels/pancake/message_handler.go`
  - `internal/channels/pancake/pancake.go`
  - `internal/channels/pancake/api_client.go`
  - `internal/channels/pancake/types.go`
  - Facebook equivalents used as a model: `internal/channels/facebook/comment_handler.go`, `post_fetcher.go`, `facebook.go`.

4. Temporary reproduction
- Created `/tmp/pancake_comment_repro.sh`, which injects a temporary package-local test file and removes it on exit.
- Before the fix, it failed because COMMENT webhooks were logged as skipped non-inbox events.
- After the fix, it passes and covers routing, keyword filtering, post context, `reply_comment`, and one-time `private_reply`.

5-6. Fix implemented
- Added `internal/channels/pancake/comment_handler.go`:
  - Feature gate for `features.comment_reply`.
  - Self/staff sender skipping.
  - Comment dedup.
  - Keyword/all filtering.
  - Echo suppression before enrichment.
  - Optional post-context enrichment.
  - Agent pipeline metadata for comment replies.
- Added `internal/channels/pancake/post_fetcher.go`:
  - TTL cache for Pancake page post lists.
  - `singleflight.Group` with a single page-level `"posts"` key to avoid API stampedes.
- Updated `webhook_handler.go`:
  - Removed the `convType != "INBOX"` drop.
  - Normalizes `post_id`, `comment_id`, `parent_id`, and post data.
  - Switches on `INBOX` vs `COMMENT` and routes accordingly.
- Updated `api_client.go`:
  - Added `ReplyComment()` with action `reply_comment`.
  - Added `PrivateReply()` with action `private_reply`.
  - Added reusable conversation-message sender.
  - Added `ListPosts()` and flexible post-list response parsing.
- Updated `pancake.go`:
  - Added `postFetcher` and `firstInboxSent` state.
  - Routes outbound messages with `pancake_mode="comment"` to comment reply flow.
  - Sends one-time private reply per sender when enabled.
- Updated `types.go` for config and webhook/API fields.
- Updated `ui/web/src/pages/channels/channel-schemas.ts` so the new Pancake options are configurable in the UI.

7-8. Verification
Passed:
- `/tmp/pancake_comment_repro.sh`
- `go test ./internal/channels/pancake`
- `go test ./internal/channels/...`
- `git diff --check`

Full suite:
- `go test ./...` still fails only in the pre-existing/unrelated `internal/http` Ollama tests (`host.docker.internal` DNS/API-base expectations), matching the baseline failure class.

UI check:
- Tried `pnpm exec tsc -b` in `ui/web`, but `pnpm` is not installed in this environment.

No repository test files were modified. The temporary reproduction test file is removed automatically and is not present in the working tree.

content.text:
Implemented the Pancake COMMENT auto-reply and first-inbox flow with minimal non-test changes.

Summary by requested phases:

1. Reading / problem restatement
- Pancake was parsing webhooks but explicitly dropping any `conversation.type != "INBOX"`, so `COMMENT` webhooks never reached the agent pipeline.
- Required behavior: route `COMMENT` events when `features.comment_reply` is true, optionally filter by keywords, optionally prepend post context, reply publicly via `reply_comment`, and optionally send one one-time DM via `private_reply` when `features.first_inbox` is true.
- Important technical details preserved: `pancake_mode="comment"`, `reply_to_comment_id`, `features.comment_reply`, `features.first_inbox`, `comment_reply_options.filter`, `comment_reply_options.keywords`, `comment_reply_options.include_post_context`, singleflight post-list fetch key behavior, and echo detection before content enrichment.

2. Running / baseline
- Baseline `go test ./...` failed in `internal/http` Ollama model tests because the environment resolves/normalizes Ollama URLs to `host.docker.internal`; this was already unrelated to Pancake.

3. Exploration
- Relevant files identified:
  - `internal/channels/pancake/webhook_handler.go`
  - `internal/channels/pancake/message_handler.go`
  - `internal/channels/pancake/pancake.go`
  - `internal/channels/pancake/api_client.go`
  - `internal/channels/pancake/types.go`
  - Facebook equivalents used as a model: `internal/channels/facebook/comment_handler.go`, `post_fetcher.go`, `facebook.go`.

4. Temporary reproduction
- Created `/tmp/pancake_comment_repro.sh`, which injects a temporary package-local test file and removes it on exit.
- Before the fix, it failed because COMMENT webhooks were logged as skipped non-inbox events.
- After the fix, it passes and covers routing, keyword filtering, post context, `reply_comment`, and one-time `private_reply`.

5-6. Fix implemented
- Added `internal/channels/pancake/comment_handler.go`:
  - Feature gate for `features.comment_reply`.
  - Self/staff sender skipping.
  - Comment dedup.
  - Keyword/all filtering.
  - Echo suppression before enrichment.
  - Optional post-context enrichment.
  - Agent pipeline metadata for comment replies.
- Added `internal/channels/pancake/post_fetcher.go`:
  - TTL cache for Pancake page post lists.
  - `singleflight.Group` with a single page-level `"posts"` key to avoid API stampedes.
- Updated `webhook_handler.go`:
  - Removed the `convType != "INBOX"` drop.
  - Normalizes `post_id`, `comment_id`, `parent_id`, and post data.
  - Switches on `INBOX` vs `COMMENT` and routes accordingly.
- Updated `api_client.go`:
  - Added `ReplyComment()` with action `reply_comment`.
  - Added `PrivateReply()` with action `private_reply`.
  - Added reusable conversation-message sender.
  - Added `ListPosts()` and flexible post-list response parsing.
- Updated `pancake.go`:
  - Added `postFetcher` and `firstInboxSent` state.
  - Routes outbound messages with `pancake_mode="comment"` to comment reply flow.
  - Sends one-time private reply per sender when enabled.
- Updated `types.go` for config and webhook/API fields.
- Updated `ui/web/src/pages/channels/channel-schemas.ts` so the new Pancake options are configurable in the UI.

7-8. Verification
Passed:
- `/tmp/pancake_comment_repro.sh`
- `go test ./internal/channels/pancake`
- `go test ./internal/channels/...`
- `git diff --check`

Full suite:
- `go test ./...` still fails only in the pre-existing/unrelated `internal/http` Ollama tests (`host.docker.internal` DNS/API-base expectations), matching the baseline failure class.

UI check:
- Tried `pnpm exec tsc -b` in `ui/web`, but `pnpm` is not installed in this environment.

No repository test files were modified. The temporary reproduction test file is removed automatically and is not present in the working tree.
